description: MCP Server for Mirage Vault — Expose already-masked vault sessions to LLM services (Claude Desktop, claude.ai, Le Chat) via MCP. Dual transport: Desktop Extension (.mcpb) for Claude Desktop, embedded SSE server for web services. Read-only, masked-only. No raw PII leaves the machine.

userStories.title: Add mcp_shared column and toggle command
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a database column and Tauri command to control which sessions are shared via MCP.
acceptanceCriteria: Idempotent ALTER TABLE migration adds `mcp_shared` INTEGER column to sessions table (default 0, meaning false)
New Tauri command `toggle_session_mcp_shared(session_id: i64, shared: bool)` that updates the mcp_shared column and updated_at timestamp
SessionSummary and SessionDetail structs gain `mcp_shared: bool` field, populated from DB queries in list_sessions and get_session
Command registered in lib.rs generate_handler![]
cargo check passes from src-tauri/
userStories.notes: Follow the existing idempotent ALTER TABLE pattern in db.rs — wrap in match with 'duplicate column name' error catch. SQLite uses INTEGER for booleans (0/1). Update the SELECT queries in list_sessions and get_session to include mcp_shared.

userStories.title: MCP Share toggle in vault UI
userStories.description: As a user, I want a toggle in the session header to control whether a session is shared via MCP.
acceptanceCriteria: SessionDetail.svelte header shows a toggle switch next to the session name/status area
Toggle label: 'Share via MCP' with a small icon (use 'link' or add a new 'share' PixelIcon if needed)
Clicking toggle calls invoke('toggle_session_mcp_shared', { sessionId, shared: newValue }) and refreshes session data
Toggle reflects current mcp_shared state from session data
SessionList.svelte session cards show a small MCP indicator icon when mcp_shared is true
pnpm check passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Add the toggle to the SessionDetail header area near the status badge. For SessionList cards, a small icon or badge like 'MCP' in accent color is sufficient. Use the Svelte 5 callback pattern for the toggle state change.

userStories.title: MCP server package scaffolding
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the TypeScript MCP server package structure set up so I can implement tools and transports.
acceptanceCriteria: New directory mirage-vault/mcp-server/ with package.json
Dependencies: @modelcontextprotocol/sdk, better-sqlite3, @types/better-sqlite3
tsconfig.json configured for Node.js (ESNext module, Node module resolution, strict mode)
Source files created: src/index-stdio.ts, src/index-sse.ts, src/core/server.ts, src/core/types.ts
src/core/server.ts creates an MCP Server instance with name 'mirage-vault' and version from package.json
src/index-stdio.ts connects the server to StdioServerTransport
Build script in package.json: tsc compiles to dist/
pnpm install && pnpm build succeeds from mcp-server/
Running `node dist/index-stdio.js` starts without crashing (exits cleanly if no DB)
userStories.notes: Use @modelcontextprotocol/sdk McpServer class and StdioServerTransport. The server.ts should export a function that creates the server (transport-agnostic). Each index file wires up the transport. Keep index-sse.ts as a stub for now — just a placeholder that logs 'SSE transport not yet implemented'.

userStories.title: Crypto compatibility module
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a TypeScript module that decrypts vault data using the same AES-256-GCM format as the Rust backend.
acceptanceCriteria: New file mcp-server/src/core/crypto.ts
Function deriveKey(passphrase: string): Buffer that returns SHA-256 hash of the passphrase as a 32-byte key

Function decrypt(key: Buffer, ciphertext: string): string that decodes base64, extracts nonce (first 12 bytes), ciphertext+tag (remaining bytes), and decrypts using AES-256-GCM via Node.js crypto module
Function decryptSafe(key: Buffer, ciphertext: string): string that returns the raw string if decryption fails (same fallback pattern as the Rust backend)
Unit test file mcp-server/src/core/crypto.test.ts with test cases for: deriveKey produces correct length, decrypt handles valid ciphertext, decryptSafe returns raw on invalid input
Tests pass with npx tsx --test src/core/crypto.test.ts
userStories.notes: The Rust format is: base64(nonce[12] || ciphertext || tag[16]). In Node.js crypto, use createDecipheriv('aes-256-gcm', key, nonce). Set auth tag with decipher.setAuthTag(tag). The tag is the LAST 16 bytes of the decoded buffer, not appended separately. Use Buffer.from(ciphertext, 'base64') to decode.

userStories.title: Database connection module
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a module that connects to the vault SQLite database in read-only mode and provides query helpers.
acceptanceCriteria: New file mcp-server/src/core/db.ts
Class VaultDB that takes a database path and encryption key (Buffer) in constructor
Opens SQLite database via better-sqlite3 in readonly mode
Method listSessions(mcpSharedOnly: boolean, includeArchived: boolean): returns session summaries with item_count and entry_count
Method getSession(sessionId: number): returns session detail with entries (raw_content decrypted via decryptSafe) and items
Method getSessionItem(sessionId: number, itemId: number): returns item masked_content and entity list (token + entity_type only, no original_value)
Method searchSessions(query: string): searches session names with LIKE, returns same format as listSessions
Method getEntityLegend(sessionId: number): returns token → entity_type mapping (no original_value)
Method getMaskedItem(itemId: number): returns item masked_content and entity list for standalone items
All session queries filter by mcp_shared = 1 when mcpSharedOnly is true
Database path defaults to platform-appropriate Tauri app data dir if not specified
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use better-sqlite3's Database constructor with { readonly: true }. For the default DB path, check process.platform: 'darwin' → ~/Library/Application Support/com.shroudai.vault/vault.db, 'linux' → ~/.local/share/com.shroudai.vault/vault.db. Session entry raw_content needs decryptSafe since it's encrypted. items.masked_content is NOT encrypted. entities.original_value is encrypted but we NEVER expose it — only serve entity_type and token.

userStories.title: Register list_sessions and search_sessions MCP tools
userStories.description: As a user, I want the LLM to discover and search my vault sessions via MCP tools.
acceptanceCriteria: New file mcp-server/src/core/tools.ts that registers all MCP tools on the server
list_sessions tool registered with optional include_archived boolean parameter
list_sessions calls VaultDB.listSessions(mcpSharedOnly=true) and returns session summaries as JSON
search_sessions tool registered with required query string parameter
search_sessions calls VaultDB.searchSessions(query) and returns matching sessions
Both tools include a note in the response: 'All sensitive data has been replaced with tokens. Tokens like [[EMAIL:abc123]] represent redacted values.'
Tool descriptions explain that content is masked/redacted
Server can be built and run: list_sessions and search_sessions respond correctly via MCP inspector or manual stdio test
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use server.tool() from @modelcontextprotocol/sdk to register tools. Each tool gets a name, description, input schema (zod or JSON Schema), and handler function. The handler receives parsed input and returns { content: [{ type: 'text', text: JSON.stringify(result) }] }. Initialize VaultDB in the server setup using env vars MIRAGE_VAULT_DB_PATH and MIRAGE_VAULT_PASSPHRASE.

userStories.title: Register get_session and get_session_item MCP tools
userStories.description: As a user, I want the LLM to fetch full session content and individual items via MCP.
acceptanceCriteria: get_session tool registered with required session_id number parameter
get_session calls VaultDB.getSession(sessionId) and returns session metadata, entries (masked), items, and entity type summary
Entity type summary shows count per type (e.g. '3 EMAIL tokens, 2 PERSON tokens') without original values
get_session_item tool registered with required session_id and item_id parameters
get_session_item calls VaultDB.getSessionItem(sessionId, itemId) and returns item masked_content, metadata, and entity list
Both tools reject requests for sessions where mcp_shared = false with a clear error message
Both tools return error for invalid session_id or item_id
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: For entity type summary in get_session, group the entity list by entity_type and count. Return something like { entity_summary: { EMAIL: 3, PERSON: 2, AMT: 1 } }. The redaction notice should be included in every response.

userStories.title: Register get_entity_legend and get_masked_item MCP tools
userStories.description: As a user, I want the LLM to understand token types and access standalone vault items.
acceptanceCriteria: get_entity_legend tool registered with required session_id parameter
get_entity_legend calls VaultDB.getEntityLegend(sessionId) and returns token → entity_type mapping grouped by type
get_entity_legend never includes original_value in the response
get_entity_legend rejects requests for sessions where mcp_shared = false
get_masked_item tool registered with required item_id parameter
get_masked_item calls VaultDB.getMaskedItem(itemId) and returns masked_content, metadata, and entity list
get_masked_item only serves items in MCP-shared sessions or items not in any session
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: For get_entity_legend, the response format should be: { legend: { EMAIL: ['[[EMAIL:a1b2c3]]', '[[EMAIL:d4e5f6]]'], PERSON: ['[[PERSON:g7h8i9]]'] } }. For get_masked_item, check if the item's session (via session_items) has mcp_shared = 1, or if the item isn't in any session (LEFT JOIN session_items IS NULL).

userStories.title: Register session MCP resources
userStories.description: As an LLM, I want to access session content via MCP resource URIs.
acceptanceCriteria: New file mcp-server/src/core/resources.ts that registers MCP resources on the server
Resource template vault://sessions/{session_id} registered via server.resource()
Reading a session resource returns the full masked session content (same as get_session tool)
Resource list (vault://sessions) returns all active MCP-shared sessions as resource entries with name and description
MIME type set to text/plain for session content
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use server.resource() with a URI template. The resource handler receives the parsed URI parameters and returns content. Resource list uses server.resource() with a static URI to list available sessions. Each session becomes a resource entry with uri, name, and description.

userStories.title: Desktop Extension manifest and packaging
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to package the MCP server as a Claude Desktop Extension (.mcpb) for easy installation.
acceptanceCriteria: manifest.json in mcp-server/ with required fields: name, version, description, icon
Manifest declares user input for vault passphrase (type: string, required: true, label: 'Vault Passphrase')
Manifest declares optional user input for custom database path (type: string, required: false, label: 'Database Path')
Manifest maps user inputs to environment variables MIRAGE_VAULT_PASSPHRASE and MIRAGE_VAULT_DB_PATH
Manifest specifies the stdio entry point (dist/index-stdio.js)
Build script: npx @anthropic-ai/mcpb pack produces a .mcpb file in mcp-server/dist/
Package script added to package.json: pnpm run package
The .mcpb file can be opened in Claude Desktop and the extension installs successfully
userStories.notes: Follow the @anthropic-ai/mcpb documentation. The manifest.json format requires: { name, version, description, icon?, inputs: [{ id, type, label, required, description }], command: { type: 'node', entry: 'dist/index-stdio.js' } }. User inputs are mapped to env vars that the server reads at startup.

userStories.title: Embedded SSE server entry point
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the SSE transport entry point so the Tauri app can run the MCP server internally.
acceptanceCriteria: mcp-server/src/index-sse.ts implements an HTTP server on 127.0.0.1:3420
SSE endpoint at /mcp handles MCP protocol over Server-Sent Events using SSEServerTransport from @modelcontextprotocol/sdk
Server reads passphrase from MIRAGE_VAULT_PASSPHRASE env var (passed by Tauri app when spawning the process)
Server binds to loopback only (127.0.0.1, not 0.0.0.0)
Server logs startup message to stderr: 'MCP SSE server listening on http://127.0.0.1:3420/mcp'
Server handles graceful shutdown on SIGTERM/SIGINT
pnpm build produces dist/index-sse.js
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use Node.js http module or express for the HTTP server. SSEServerTransport from @modelcontextprotocol/sdk handles the SSE protocol. The Tauri app will spawn this as a sidecar process with the passphrase passed via env var. Port 3420 chosen to avoid conflicts with common dev ports.

userStories.title: Tauri sidecar management for embedded SSE server
userStories.description: As a user, I want the MCP SSE server to start automatically when I unlock my vault and stop when I close the app.
acceptanceCriteria: New Tauri command start_mcp_server() that spawns the SSE server as a child process (node dist/index-sse.js) with MIRAGE_VAULT_PASSPHRASE and MIRAGE_VAULT_DB_PATH env vars
New Tauri command stop_mcp_server() that kills the child process
New Tauri command get_mcp_server_status() that returns whether the server is running and on which port
SSE server process is spawned after successful init_encryption (vault unlock)
SSE server process is killed on clear_encryption_key (vault lock) and on app exit
All three commands registered in lib.rs generate_handler![]
cargo check passes from src-tauri/
userStories.notes: Use std::process::Command to spawn the Node process. Store the Child handle in a Mutex<Option<Child>> managed state. The mcp-server dist/ must be accessible — either bundled as a Tauri resource or referenced by path. Pass the passphrase via the child process environment, not via command line args (security).

userStories.title: MCP server status indicator and .mcpb download in vault UI
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see whether the MCP server is running and download the Claude Desktop extension from the vault app.
acceptanceCriteria: Settings view gains an 'MCP Server' section
Section shows server status: 'Running on port 3420' (green dot) or 'Stopped' (gray dot), refreshed via invoke('get_mcp_server_status')
Start/Stop button to manually control the SSE server
'Connect to Claude Desktop' subsection with a download button
Download button saves the bundled .mcpb file to the user's Downloads folder via Tauri file dialog
Brief instruction text: 'Double-click the downloaded file to install in Claude Desktop'
Instruction text for web services: 'Add this URL as an MCP server in claude.ai or Le Chat: http://127.0.0.1:3420/mcp'
pnpm check passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: The .mcpb file should be bundled as a Tauri resource at build time. Use tauri::api::path to locate the bundled resource. The download uses tauri_plugin_dialog for the save dialog. For the status indicator, poll get_mcp_server_status on mount and after start/stop actions.

userStories.title: README and developer documentation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need documentation for building, testing, and configuring the MCP server.
acceptanceCriteria: README.md in mcp-server/ with: project overview, build instructions, environment variables reference, available MCP tools and their parameters, example Claude Desktop config snippet, example SSE connection for web services
Claude Desktop config example shows both stdio (manual) and .mcpb (extension) setup
Web services config example shows the localhost URL
All six MCP tools documented with parameter types, descriptions, and example responses
Security section explaining: read-only access, masked-only content, mcp_shared gate, loopback binding
userStories.notes: Keep the README focused and practical. Developers should be able to get started in under 5 minutes. Include the JSON snippet for claude_desktop_config.json for manual setup as an alternative to the .mcpb extension.
